headlines.headline: Russia Strikes Odesa and Dnipro in Overnight Attacks
headlines.summary: Russian forces targeted Ukrainian cities of Odesa and Dnipro with missiles and drones, damaging residential buildings and injuring dozens, while Ukrainian drone strikes killed two in Russia.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Has High Expectations for Putin's China Visit
headlines.summary: Russia expressed serious expectations for President Putin's upcoming trip to China this week, where he will meet Xi Jinping to discuss developing their "privileged partnership" amid the Iran war and Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Hungary to Hold Minority Rights Consultations
headlines.summary: Kyiv and Budapest agreed to talks about Ukraine's ethnic Hungarian minority rights this week, seeking to rebuild ties following Hungary's government change.

headlines.headline: Putin Faces Difficult Choices as Sanctions Bite, Estonia Spy Chief Says
headlines.summary: Estonia's foreign intelligence chief said Russia has few good options in Ukraine as Western sanctions chip away at its resources while its forces cannot advance significantly on the battlefield.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Jump on Iran War Re-Escalation Fears
headlines.summary: Oil prices rose again as diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran war appeared to stall, with Tehran submitting a revised peace proposal while the US warned of potential renewed military action.

headlines.headline: Israel-Hezbollah fighting continues despite truce extension
headlines.summary: Israel carried out airstrikes in southern Lebanon while Hezbollah announced new attacks on Israeli forces, continuing the conflict despite a U.S.-backed truce extension. Lebanon death toll nears 3,000.

headlines.headline: Dollar retreats as oil falls on report of US-Iran sanctions waiver
headlines.summary: The dollar slipped against major currencies as oil prices fell after an Iranian news report indicated the US may waive sanctions on Iranian crude. 10-year Treasury yields withdrew from 15-month highs.

perspectives.angle: US sanctions waiver for Russian oil purchases expires without renewal
perspectives.summary: The Treasury Department allowed the waiver permitting countries to purchase Russian oil cargoes stranded at sea to expire on May 16. Treasury Secretary Bessent had previously said there were no plans to renew the authorization.

headlines.headline: Croatia Rejects Proposed Israeli Ambassador Over Government Policies
headlines.summary: Croatian President Milanović says the nominee will not receive approval, citing actions of the current Israeli government and a diplomatic protocol dispute.

headlines.headline: Merkel Criticizes EU for Not Talking to Russia on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel said the EU should maintain dialogue with Russia, though she declined to lead such talks herself, in a rare political intervention.

headlines.headline: Syria Joins G7 Finance Sessions in Paris in Sign of Legitimization
headlines.summary: Syria will participate in closed-door G7 finance minister meetings in Paris, a sign of its growing international status less than two years after the fall of Assad.

headlines.headline: Iran Establishes New Body to Manage Strait of Hormuz Amid Stalled Talks
headlines.summary: Iran formed the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) to manage the vital shipping route as peace negotiations with the US remained deadlocked.

perspectives.angle: Iran Forms New Body to Manage Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: Iran's top security council announced the formation of a new authority to manage the strategic Strait of Hormuz, the vital shipping chokepoint that has been closed during the conflict.

headlines.headline: Greenland PM Tells US Envoy: Self-Determination Cannot Be Negotiated
headlines.summary: Greenland Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen met with US special envoy Jeff Landry and reaffirmed that Greenland self-determination is not for negotiation.

headlines.headline: Senior Islamic State Leader Killed in US-Nigeria Joint Operation
headlines.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, a senior Islamic State leader and head of the General Directorate of States, was killed along with several lieutenants in a strike on his compound in Lake Chad Basin. He was linked to the 2018 Dapchi schoolgirls kidnapping.

perspectives.angle: Senior Islamic State leader killed in US-Nigeria joint operation
perspectives.summary: US and Nigerian forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described by President Trump as ISIS's second-in-command globally, in a precision strike on his compound in the Lake Chad Basin. Nigerian President Tinubu called it the most consequential counterterrorism outcome since regional operations began in 2015.

headlines.headline: USAID Cuts Linked to Surge in Violence Across Africa, Study Finds
headlines.summary: A study published in Science journal found that the abrupt dissolution of USAID last year was followed by a significant increase in violence in African countries that had historically received the most support from the agency.

headlines.headline: Russian Drone Strikes Chinese-Linked Cargo Ship Near Odesa
headlines.summary: A Russian Shahed drone hit a Chinese-owned bulk carrier in the Black Sea near Odesa, injuring no one but damaging two civilian vessels. The incident comes just before Putin's visit to Beijing to deepen ties with China.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Troops Nearly Cut Off in Northern Pokrovsk
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces in northern Pokrovsk are "almost cut off" as Russian drones dominate logistics routes, making resupply, evacuations, and troop rotations nearly impossible, according to Ukraine's 7th Rapid Response Corps.

headlines.headline: Zelensky's Former Chief of Staff Released on $3 Million Bail
headlines.summary: Andriy Yermak, former head of Ukraine's presidential office, was released on $3.1 million bail amid a major corruption probe involving money laundering tied to the construction of a luxury cottage complex near Kyiv.

headlines.headline: Drone Strike Hits UAE Nuclear Plant as Iran Ceasefire Teeters
headlines.summary: A drone strike targeted the United Arab Emirates Barakah nuclear power plant, sparking a fire on its perimeter. The attack comes as diplomatic efforts to extend the fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran face mounting challenges.

perspectives.angle: UAE Blames Iran for Drone Strike Near Barakah Nuclear Power Plant
perspectives.summary: The United Arab Emirates accused Iran or its proxies of launching a drone that sparked a fire just outside the Barakah nuclear facility. No radiation leaks were reported, but the incident marks a dangerous escalation as Iran war ceasefire talks remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Fall, Wall Street Hits Record as Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Oil prices dropped sharply and US stocks surged to record highs after Iran announced the Strait of Hormuz was open again for commercial tankers. The S&P 500 jumped 1.2% as markets rallied on hopes for de-escalation.

perspectives.angle: Stocks fall and oil rises after Trump warns Iran that clock is ticking
perspectives.summary: Financial markets reacted to escalating Iran tensions, with equities declining and crude prices climbing as the US president issued warnings about the Iranian situation.

headlines.headline: Macron in Kenya for Africa Summit as France Seeks New Continental Partnership
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron kicked off a visit to Kenya for the Africa Forward Summit, showcasing France shift from colonial-era dominance toward partnership with African nations. The summit comes amid Frances withdrawal of troops from West Africa.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo to Release First Encyclical on Artificial Intelligence May 25
headlines.summary: Pope Leo will release his first in-depth document on May 25, expected to address the rise of artificial intelligence and challenges to workers rights. The encyclical Magnifica Humanitas will be launched with Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo XIV to release first encyclical on AI with Anthropic co-founder
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo XIV will launch his first encyclical on May 25 addressing human dignity in the AI era, joined by Anthropic co-founder. The document will tackle ethical challenges posed by artificial intelligence.

headlines.headline: China Economy Slows Sharply as Industrial Output and Retail Sales Disappoint
headlines.summary: China growth lost momentum in April, with industrial output cooling and retail sales sinking to over three-year lows as the second-largest economy grapples with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand.

perspectives.angle: China economic growth stalls as output and retail sales miss forecasts
perspectives.summary: Industrial output and retail sales sharply undershot expectations in April as the world second-biggest economy wrestled with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand.

headlines.headline: Kenya Paralyzed by Nationwide Fuel Protests as Iran War Drives Price Surge
headlines.summary: Protests over fuel price hikes erupted in Kenyan towns, with a nationwide public transport strike stranding commuters and forcing many to walk to work. Demonstrators lit bonfires and barricaded roads amid anger over record fuel costs.

perspectives.angle: Comoros suspends fuel price hikes after deadly Iran-war protests
perspectives.summary: The East African island nation backed down on fuel price increases triggered by the Iran conflict after protests turned deadly across the archipelago.

headlines.headline: Global Bond Rout Deepens as Iran War Stokes Worldwide Inflation Fears
headlines.summary: Government bonds from Tokyo to New York extended losses as rising energy prices from the Iran conflict fanned inflation fears and stoked investor wagers on rate hikes from central banks around the world.

perspectives.angle: Global Bond Rout Deepens as Iran War Fuels Inflation Fears
perspectives.summary: Bonds from Tokyo to New York extended losses as rising energy prices from the ongoing Middle East war fanned inflation fears and stoked investor wagers on rate hikes from global central banks. The yield spike poses risks to unprepared equity markets.

headlines.headline: Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship Docks in Netherlands for Disinfection After Deadly Outbreak
headlines.summary: A luxury polar explorer ship arrived at Rotterdam port where authorities quarantined 27 remaining crew and planned to cremate a German passenger who died. The MV Hondius had been at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak during its seven-week voyage.

headlines.headline: IEA Warns Commercial Oil Inventories Depleting Rapidly
headlines.summary: IEA chief Fatih Birol said commercial oil inventories are depleting rapidly with only weeks worth left due to the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure to shipping.

headlines.headline: Cuba Denies Seeking Russian Drones, Accuses US of Building Case for Intervention
headlines.summary: Havana's foreign minister rejected a report that Cuba acquired attack drones from Russia, calling it a "fraudulent case" to justify US aggression.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Wins Eurovision 2026, Israel Takes Second Place
headlines.summary: Bulgaria's Dara won Eurovision 2026 with her song "Bangaranga," while Israel finished second with 343 points in a competition held under the shadow of regional conflict.

headlines.headline: Israel Built Secret Military Bases in Iraq Before Iran War
headlines.summary: Israel spent over a year preparing covert military sites in western Iraq before launching operations against Iran, according to New York Times reporting. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.

headlines.headline: Israeli Navy Intercepts Gaza-Bound Flotilla in International Waters
headlines.summary: Israeli naval forces boarded multiple civilian vessels from the Global Sumud Flotilla carrying humanitarian aid activists, detaining participants and diverting ships to Ashdod port in enforcement of the Gaza maritime blockade.

headlines.headline: Belarus Begins Nuclear Weapons Training Exercise With Russia
headlines.summary: Under the leadership of the Belarusian Chief of General Staff, military units began training for combat use of nuclear weapons and nuclear support, working with Russian forces on delivery and preparation of nuclear munitions.

headlines.headline: Putin Agrees to NATO-Style Security Guarantees for Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin agreed at his summit with Trump that the US and European allies could offer Ukraine Article 5-like security protections as part of a potential peace deal. Trump envoy Steve Witkoff called it game-changing and a major shift in Russia's position.

headlines.headline: Major Media Outlets Demand Israel Allow Independent Journalists Into Gaza
headlines.summary: Leaders of major media organizations are urging Israel to lift a ban preventing foreign journalists from independently entering Gaza, citing press freedom and public's right to know.

headlines.headline: Australian Workers to Strike at Inpex LNG Facility Amid Global Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: A union in Australia announced workers will strike at Inpex's Ichthys LNG facility from May 27, a move that could worsen already tight global energy supplies as Middle East tensions continue.

headlines.headline: Chinese scientists land on disputed Sandy Cay reef as Philippines tensions escalate
headlines.summary: Beijing researchers landed on the uninhabited South China Sea sandbank over the weekend in the latest assertion of sovereignty claims, amid ongoing territorial disputes with Manila.

headlines.headline: Austrian jets intercept US military aircraft two days in a row amid airspace tensions
headlines.summary: Austria scrambled fighter jets to intercept US military planes for the second consecutive day, highlighting growing friction over military flight operations in European airspace.

headlines.headline: Israels F-35s to get external fuel tanks after extensive Iran combat operations
headlines.summary: Fresh off combat operations against Iran, Israel is boosting the F-35I Adirs endurance with external fuel tanks to reduce dependence on tanker aircraft for long-range missions.

headlines.headline: Middle East oil crisis has already cost global businesses 25 billion dollars
headlines.summary: The economic impact of disrupted Hormuz shipments is mounting rapidly, with Brent crude above 111 and companies worldwide scrambling to secure alternative supply chains.

perspectives.angle: $200 Oil No Longer Unthinkable as Middle East Supply Collapses
perspectives.summary: Middle East oil exports have fallen by 7-10 million bpd, creating severe physical shortages. Analysts warn prices could surge to $150-$200+ per barrel if disruptions persist, with recovery expected to be slow even if conflict ends.

headlines.headline: WHO Declares Ebola Global Health Emergency in Congo-Uganda Outbreak
headlines.summary: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern after more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths. The CDC confirmed Americans are among those affected.

perspectives.angle: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda a Global Health Emergency
perspectives.summary: The World Health Organization declared a public health emergency of international concern after over 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths from the Bundibugyo virus variant, which has no approved treatments or vaccines. Cases have spread from Congo to Ugandas capital Kampala.

headlines.headline: China Earthquake Kills Two, Forces Thousands to Evacuate
headlines.summary: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake in China's southwest Guangxi region killed two people and forced more than 7,000 in the city of Liuzhou to evacuate. Authorities warn of transport disruptions as search and rescue operations continue.

headlines.headline: Cuba Seeks Russian Drones, Eyes Guantanamo Attack
headlines.summary: US intelligence reports Cuba has purchased 300+ Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay and Key West, with Iranian military advisors now in Havana.

headlines.headline: Turkish Arms Subsidiary Rebrands After Israel Sales Backlash
headlines.summary: Repkon USA, a Turkish-owned defense contractor, rebranded to Paligen Technologies after revelations it would supply bomb bodies for Israel, sparking protests in Istanbul and parliamentary inquiries in Turkey.

headlines.headline: Ethiopia-Eritrea War Fears as Tigray Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: TPLF leader Debretsion Gebremichael reclaimed presidency of Tigray region, challenging the interim administration. Ethiopian fighter jets seen over Mekelle as fears grow of renewed conflict with Eritrea.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Touts Shift in Battlefield Balance After Moscow Strike
headlines.summary: Following Ukraines largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year, Zelensky announced a shift in momentum and promised more deep strikes as Russia downed over 3,000 drones in the past week.

headlines.headline: Hungary Opposition Leads Orbán for First Time in 15 Years
headlines.summary: Péter Magyars Tisza party leads Viktor Orbáns Fidesz by 12 points ahead of April elections in what Politico calls the EUs most consequential election of 2026.

headlines.headline: Climate Change Reducing Oxygen Levels in Rivers Worldwide, Scientists Find
headlines.summary: A new study reveals that global warming is systematically reducing dissolved oxygen in rivers across the globe, creating expanding "dead zones" that threaten aquatic ecosystems and drinking water supplies.

headlines.headline: Oil prices climb after drone attack on UAE nuclear plant
headlines.summary: Oil prices extended gains as efforts to end the US-Iran conflict appeared stalled following a drone attack on a UAE nuclear power plant and President Trump prepared to discuss military options on Iran.

headlines.headline: German prosecutors expose Russian sanctions-busting network supplying military tech
headlines.summary: A four-year investigation uncovered a Lübeck-based trading firm that routed dual-use components through Turkey into Russia, violating Western sanctions to supply the Kremlin war machine.

headlines.headline: Brent crude breaks $111 as Gulf drone attacks and Hormuz fears spark shortage concerns
headlines.summary: Oil prices surged in early Asian trading after drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia, combined with failed Iran diplomacy, raised fears of global supply disruptions. WTI hit $108.20 while Brent climbed to $111.50.

headlines.headline: Xi and Trump agree Iran must not obtain nuclear weapons in strategic shift on Gulf security
headlines.summary: China and the US reached a rare point of agreement on Iran policy during Trumps China visit, with both leaders signaling that Tehrans nuclear ambitions must be contained, reshaping Middle East power dynamics.

headlines.headline: NATO Defense Ministers Meet Without US Leadership Amid Questions
headlines.summary: European allies downplayed concerns after US Defense Secretary Hegseth skipped the NATO gathering in Brussels, marking the second consecutive high-level US absence. Germany and Iceland insisted the alliance remains strong despite the unusual US no-shows.

headlines.headline: Drone Strike Hits UAE Barakah Nuclear Plant Amid Iran War Deadlock
headlines.summary: A drone strike caused a fire at the UAE sole nuclear power plant, with Emirati officials investigating the source and vowing response to what they called a terrorist attack.

perspectives.angle: Drone strikes UAE nuclear plant as US and Iran signal readiness to resume war
perspectives.summary: A drone attack targeted a UAE nuclear facility amid escalating regional tensions, with both Washington and Tehran indicating they are prepared for renewed conflict.

headlines.headline: G7 Finance Chiefs Convene in Paris to Tackle Trade Imbalances
headlines.summary: Finance ministers from G7 nations meet in Paris to coordinate critical raw material supplies and address global economic tensions amid geopolitical differences threatening group cohesion.

headlines.headline: Israel to Establish Defense Offices in Former UNRWA East Jerusalem Compound
headlines.summary: Israel plans to set up defense offices in the former UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem after demolishing structures, a move the UN agency called a violation of international law.

perspectives.angle: Israel to Build Defense Offices in Former UNRWA Jerusalem Compound
perspectives.summary: Israel cabinet approved building a defense compound on the site of recently demolished UNRWA premises in East Jerusalem. The UN had called the demolition a violation of international law.

headlines.headline: Russia Destroyed Over 3,000 Ukrainian Drones in Past Week
headlines.summary: Russian defense ministry reports intercepting and destroying at least 3,124 Ukrainian drones over the past week, as Ukraine continues its drone campaign against Russian targets.

headlines.headline: UAE Pivots from Oil to AI as Post-War Economic Strategy
headlines.summary: With its OPEC era over, the UAE is betting big on artificial intelligence to build economic bridges to the Global South, bolstered by cutting-edge Nvidia chip deliveries. The strategy has been tested by Iranian attacks on Emirati data centers during the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Signals Readiness to Resume Dialogue with Europe on Ukraine War
headlines.summary: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Moscow is open to restarting dialogue with Europe over the war in Ukraine, following a major Ukrainian drone attack on the Moscow region. The comments come as European officials debate future engagement with Moscow amid uncertainty over US peace efforts.

headlines.headline: Philippine VP Sara Duterte Faces Senate Impeachment Trial
headlines.summary: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a senate impeachment trial on charges including bribery, corruption, and threatening the president's life. The outcome could reshape the 2028 presidential race and determine the Duterte clan's future in national politics.

headlines.headline: G7 Finance Ministers Meet in Paris Amid Trade Tensions and Iran War Fallout
headlines.summary: Finance ministers from the Group of Seven nations convene in Paris to address global economic imbalances and coordinate critical raw material supplies. Geopolitical differences over the Iran conflict threaten to test the group's unity as the Strait of Hormuz blockade disrupts energy markets.

headlines.headline: German Finance Minister Calls G7 the Right Forum to End Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil says the G7 is the appropriate platform to discuss ending the war in Iran, which along with the Strait of Hormuz blockade poses a serious threat to the global economy. The statement comes ahead of the G7 finance ministers meeting in Paris.

headlines.headline: CDC Aiding Withdrawal of Americans Potentially Exposed to Ebola in Congo
headlines.summary: The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is helping withdraw Americans who may have been exposed to Ebola during the outbreak in Congo and Uganda. A CDC official stressed that the risk to the United States remains low.

headlines.headline: Washington Post: CDC Readies Team to Respond to Ebola Outbreak
headlines.summary: The CDC is preparing a response team as the WHO declares the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a public health emergency. Health officials are coordinating with international partners to contain the spread of the virus.

headlines.headline: Taiwan President responds to Trump summit: 'We will not be sacrificed'
headlines.summary: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te pushed back after Trump raised doubts about US arms sales and willingness to defend the island following his Beijing summit with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan president defends US arms purchases after Trump calls them a bargaining chip
perspectives.summary: Taiwan's leader pushed back against criticism from former President Trump, defending the island's military purchases from the US as essential for deterrence against China.

headlines.headline: Senate parliamentarian blocks $1 billion White House security funding
headlines.summary: The Senate parliamentarian ruled that Republican efforts to allocate funding for Trump's proposed White House ballroom project cannot proceed through budget reconciliation.

perspectives.angle: Senate Parliamentarian Blocks $1 Billion White House Security Proposal
perspectives.summary: The Senate parliamentarian ruled that a $1 billion proposal for White House security funding, including a proposed ballroom, cannot proceed under budget reconciliation rules. The decision deals a blow to administration spending plans.

headlines.headline: Political executions surge in Iran since war began, UN verifies at least 32
headlines.summary: The United Nations has verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners in Iran since the US-Israel war began on February 28.

headlines.headline: IDF Extends Death Penalty Law to West Bank Palestinians Convicted of Terror
headlines.summary: Israel Defense Forces Central Command chief has applied a new death penalty law to Palestinians in the West Bank convicted of terrorism, expanding capital punishment beyond Israel proper.

headlines.headline: Argentina Retires A-4 Fightinghawks After Six Decades
headlines.summary: Argentina has finally stood down its A-4 Skyhawks, the type's first export operator, after 60 years of service as the country introduces its first F-16s.

headlines.headline: Norway Arrests Chinese Man for Spying
headlines.summary: Norway's interior security service has arrested a Chinese national on suspicion of espionage, marking another case of alleged Chinese intelligence activity in Europe.

headlines.headline: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak in Congo and Uganda Global Health Emergency
headlines.summary: The World Health Organization designated the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern, with at least 80 deaths reported and no approved vaccines for this strain.

perspectives.angle: WHO Declares Ebola Outbreak a Global Health Emergency
perspectives.summary: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in DR Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern after 88 deaths. The outbreak involves the rare Bundibugyo strain which has no approved vaccine or treatment.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Drone Attack Kills Four in Moscow's Biggest Strike in Over a Year
headlines.summary: At least four people were killed after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack on Moscow in more than a year, demonstrating Kyiv's ability to strike targets over 300 miles from the border despite dense Russian air defenses.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Launches Largest Drone Attack on Moscow in Over a Year
perspectives.summary: Ukraine launched its biggest drone attack on the Russian capital in more than a year, killing at least four people including three in the Moscow region. Russia reported over 1,000 Ukrainian drones downed in 24 hours.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding dozens
perspectives.summary: A major Ukrainian drone offensive targeted multiple locations inside Russia, resulting in casualties and demonstrating Kyiv's continued capability to strike deep into enemy territory.

headlines.headline: Cuba Accuses US of Fabricating Drone Threat Claims
headlines.summary: Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez dismissed US intelligence reports about Cuban drone acquisitions from Russia and Iran as a "fraudulent case" designed to justify sanctions and potential military intervention.

headlines.headline: Moldova Condemns Russian Offer of Citizenship to Transdniestria Separatists
headlines.summary: Moldovan leaders denounced President Putin's offer of simplified Russian citizenship for the pro-Russian Transdniestria region as a threat to sovereignty, amid escalating tensions over the separatist enclave.

perspectives.angle: Moldova Condemns Russian Offer of Citizenship to Separatist Region
perspectives.summary: Moldovan leaders denounced Russian President Vladimir Putin's offer of simplified Russian citizenship for the pro-Russian Transdniestria separatist enclave as a threat, contemplating measures to counteract it.

headlines.headline: Iran Excludes Star Forward Azmoun from Preliminary World Cup Squad
headlines.summary: Iran's national team left out attacking talisman Sardar Azmoun from its preliminary World Cup squad as the team prepares for the tournament amid ongoing diplomatic tensions over participation.

headlines.headline: Air Show Collision Under Investigation at Idaho Air Force Base
headlines.summary: Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho is investigating an aircraft collision during an air show performance, with no immediate reports of casualties.

headlines.headline: Bond Yield Spike Poses Risk to Unprepared Equities Market
headlines.summary: Rising benchmark yields threaten equity valuations as companies and consumers face higher borrowing costs, with investors warning that markets may be underestimating the impact on growth and corporate profits.

headlines.headline: China agrees to buy at least $17 billion in US agricultural products annually
headlines.summary: The White House announced that China has committed to purchasing at least $17 billion in US agricultural products annually following the Beijing summit between Trump and Xi Jinping. The agreement represents a significant trade concession from Beijing amid ongoing tariff tensions.

perspectives.angle: China agrees to boost US agricultural imports after Trump-Xi summit
perspectives.summary: Beijing committed to increasing purchases of American farm products including beef and poultry following high-level talks between Chinese and US leaders.

perspectives.angle: China Signals Tariff Cuts After Trump-Xi Summit
perspectives.summary: China and the US agreed to expand agricultural trade through tariff reductions and address non-tariff barriers following the Beijing summit. The agreement marks a potential de-escalation in trade tensions between the two largest economies.

headlines.headline: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace negotiations stall
headlines.summary: President Trump issued a stark warning to Iran that 'the clock is ticking' as ceasefire negotiations remain deadlocked. The US and Iran remain at impasse over ending the war, with neither side agreeing to terms for a permanent peace deal.

perspectives.angle: Trump Warns Iran "Clock Is Ticking" as Peace Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: President Trump issued a stern warning to Iran as peace negotiations show signs of stalling, with Iranian media reporting the US has not made concrete concessions in response to Tehran's latest proposals.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia intercepts drones entering from Iraq amid regional tensions
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia announced it intercepted and destroyed three drones that entered its airspace from Iraq. The incident comes as Gulf states face escalating threats from the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu faces potential political downfall as Israeli government teeters
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Binyamin Netanyahu may be facing his final days in power as political pressures mount. The Economist analyzes whether the long-serving leader can survive the current crisis.

headlines.headline: Gulf states push to restore maritime navigation as Iran faces trust deficit
headlines.summary: A Gulf official told Al Arabiya that Iran must work to restore trust with GCC states. Regional powers are stepping up efforts to secure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions.

perspectives.angle: Gulf States Edge Closer to Direct Confrontation with Iran
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia and UAE have conducted military strikes inside Iran in retaliation for Iranian attacks, marking a shift from proxy conflicts to direct engagement. Kuwait also arrested IRGC members plotting attacks on Bubiyan Island.

headlines.headline: Air Force Base Locked Down After Idaho Air Show Incident
headlines.summary: Emergency crews responded to an aircraft incident during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho. Witnesses reported mid-air collision involving Navy jets with parachutes seen descending.

perspectives.angle: US Navy Growler Jets Collide During Idaho Air Show
perspectives.summary: Two EA-18G Growler electronic warfare jets collided during an air show in Idaho, with all four crew members ejecting safely as the aircraft plummeted to the ground.

headlines.headline: Republicans Vie to Replace Defeated Senator Cassidy in Louisiana
headlines.summary: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy was defeated in the primary after his impeachment vote against Trump. Representative Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming advance to the runoff.

headlines.headline: Cuba seeking Russian drones, military equipment as US warns of Guantanamo threat
headlines.summary: US intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay, Key West, and US military vessels. Cuban officials are studying how Iran has resisted US military pressure.

headlines.headline: New Epstein victims emerge in French investigation
headlines.summary: Paris prosecutor says around 10 new victims have come forward since France launched a special probe into Jeffrey Epstein's network in February. Investigators are working to interview victims located abroad.

headlines.headline: Israel Economy Contracts in Q1 as Iran War Takes Toll
headlines.summary: Israels economy shrank at an annualized 3.3% in the first quarter, hit by the war with Iran, but growth is expected to recover if the conflict does not reignite.

perspectives.angle: Report Reveals Israel Ran Second Covert Military Base in Iraq During Iran War
perspectives.summary: A previously undisclosed Israeli military outpost in western Iraq came to light after a Bedouin shepherd was killed near the site. The bases were used for air support, refueling and medical evacuation operations during the 12-day war with Iran.

headlines.headline: US Intelligence Warns of Cuban Drone Threat to Guantanamo Bay
headlines.summary: Classified intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 military drones and discussed plans to attack the US base at Guantanamo Bay, US naval vessels, and potentially Key West, Florida.

headlines.headline: China Commits to B Annual US Agricultural Purchases Through 2028
headlines.summary: China has agreed to purchase at least  billion of US agricultural products annually through 2028, the White House announced, offering stability to American farmers after Phase One trade deal.

headlines.headline: Trump to Hold Situation Room Meeting Tuesday on Iran Military Options
headlines.summary: President Trump is expected to meet with top national security advisers on Tuesday to discuss military options regarding Iran, as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled.

headlines.headline: IDF Reports Shortage of 12,000 Troops Amid Multi-Front War
headlines.summary: The Israeli military announced a critical manpower gap of approximately 12,000 soldiers, including 6,000-7,500 combat troops, as it operates across seven different arenas. Officials warn the shortage will worsen if mandatory service is reduced to 30 months.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Signals Interest in Restoring Dialogue With Europe
headlines.summary: Russian spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Moscow could restart dialogue with Europe over the war in Ukraine, speaking after the large-scale Ukrainian drone attack on Moscow. The remarks come as European officials debate future engagement with Russia.

headlines.headline: Train crash kills 8 in Bangkok, driver charged with negligence
headlines.summary: A freight train collided with a public bus at a rail crossing in central Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring 32. Thai police have charged the train driver with negligence as investigations continue.

headlines.headline: Islamist attack on Nigerian military school kills 17 police trainees
headlines.summary: Suspected Islamist militants attacked a specialized military school in Yobe state, killing at least 17 police officers in training. The attack marks a major escalation in northeast Nigeria's ongoing insurgency.

headlines.headline: New York's largest commuter rail system shuts down in first strike since 1994
headlines.summary: The Long Island Rail Road ceased operations as five unions walked off the job after months of stalled contract negotiations. Governor Hochul pleaded with unions to resume talks before Monday's rush hour, as 250,000 daily commuters face disruption.

perspectives.angle: New York's Long Island Rail Road shut down by union strike
perspectives.summary: North America's largest commuter rail system halted operations Saturday as unions launched a strike after stalled contract negotiations, disrupting travel for thousands of commuters.

headlines.headline: Peru electoral body pledges to fix voting flaws before June presidential runoff
headlines.summary: Peru's top electoral authority acknowledged flaws that delayed April's first-round results by a month. The runoff between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez is scheduled for June 7, following weeks of political uncertainty.

perspectives.angle: Peru Confirms June 7 Presidential Runoff Between Fujimori and Sánchez
perspectives.summary: Peruvian electoral authorities officially confirmed the presidential runoff election scheduled for June 7, setting up a contest between Keiko Fujimori and Pedro Sánchez.

headlines.headline: USS Ford Aircraft Carrier Returns Home After 11-Month Iran War Deployment
headlines.summary: The USS Ford aircraft carrier has returned to its home port after an 11-month deployment that supported operations related to the Iran conflict and the capture of Venezuelan leader Maduro. The carrier was one of the longest deployments for a US aircraft carrier in recent years.

perspectives.angle: USS Gerald R. Ford Returns After Record 11-Month Deployment Supporting Iran War and Venezuela Operation
perspectives.summary: The worlds largest aircraft carrier returned to Virginia after 326 days at sea, the longest deployment since Vietnam War, having supported combat operations against Iran and the capture of Venezuelas Maduro. The crew received the Presidential Unit Citation for outstanding performance in combat.

headlines.headline: Car Rams Into Pedestrians in Italy, Injuring Eight People
headlines.summary: A driver plowed into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight people, four seriously. Passers-by chased and stopped the attacker. The incident is being investigated for possible terrorist motives.

headlines.headline: More Than 50 Schoolchildren Including Toddlers Kidnapped in Nigeria
headlines.summary: Armed gunmen have kidnapped more than 50 schoolchildren, including toddlers, in an attack on a school in Borno state, Nigeria. No group has claimed responsibility for the attack, which has devastated the local community.

perspectives.angle: Over 50 Schoolchildren Including Toddlers Kidnapped in Nigeria
perspectives.summary: Gunmen abducted more than 50 children, many aged 2-5 years old, from three schools in Mussa, Borno state. Attackers used children as human shields while fleeing on motorbikes. No group has claimed responsibility, though the attack bears hallmarks of Boko Haram activity in the region.

headlines.headline: US Navy plans Trump-class nuclear battleships to counter China
headlines.summary: The US Navy announced plans to build up to 15 nuclear-powered Trump-class battleships by 2055, dubbed the "Golden Fleet," though analysts question their vulnerability to anti-ship missiles and whether the US has sufficient industrial capacity.

perspectives.angle: Nuclear-Powered Trump Class Battleships to Reverse Navy Decision
perspectives.summary: Chief of Naval Operations says abandoning nuclear-powered surface combatants was one of the worst decisions the service ever made, as the new Trump class battleships will feature nuclear propulsion.

headlines.headline: SBU strikes Moscow defense plants and Crimea airbase in coordinated drone raid
headlines.summary: Ukrainian SBU and armed forces hit the Angstrem semiconductor plant in Zelenograd, the Moscow oil refinery, and military airfields in Crimea in one of the largest drone operations against Russian military-industrial targets.

headlines.headline: US to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 Brothers to the Rescue shootdown
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department will formally charge former Cuban president Raúl Castro next Wednesday in Miami for the 1996 downing of two civilian aircraft operated by the exile group Brothers to the Rescue, which killed four volunteers.

headlines.headline: Iranian president thanks neighbors for blocking territory use against Iran
headlines.summary: Iran President Masoud Pezeshkian praised neighboring countries for preventing "misuse of their territory" against Iran during a meeting with Pakistan interior minister, amid ongoing regional tensions and ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: AIPAC leads record spending to unseat Thomas Massie in House primary
headlines.summary: Pro-Israel groups are pouring unprecedented funds into defeating Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) in what has become the most expensive House primary in history, with AIPAC and aligned super PACs spending millions on attack ads.

perspectives.angle: GOP Rep. Massie Introduces AIPAC Act Targeting Foreign Lobbying Days Before Primary
perspectives.summary: Rep. Thomas Massie, the harshest Republican critic of Israel in Congress, introduced legislation aimed at foreign lobbying efforts just days before his Kentucky primary. The bill targets AIPAC and similar organizations.

headlines.headline: US to Weigh Action Against China Over Industrial Overcapacity
headlines.summary: US Trade Ambassador says President Trump will be presented with options if investigations determine Chinese industrial overcapacity harms American interests, as trade tensions persist.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Pushes Back Against Chinese Business Complaints Over New Rules
headlines.summary: Chinese businesses issued a blunt warning to President Prabowo that tougher regulations are hurting investor confidence, revealing tensions between Jakarta push for resource control and foreign capital.

headlines.headline: White House to Host Mass Prayer Event Promoting Christian Origins of US
headlines.summary: Trump administration officials including Pete Hegseth, Marco Rubio, and Mike Johnson will address a mass prayer meeting in Washington as part of US 250th anniversary celebrations, drawing criticism from those who see it as Christian nationalism.

headlines.headline: ASML-Tata Sign Semiconductor Deal During Modi Netherlands Visit
headlines.summary: India Prime Minister Modi witnessed the signing of a deal between Dutch tech giant ASML and Tata Electronics to build and scale a semiconductor plant in Gujarat, advancing India chip manufacturing ambitions.

headlines.headline: US Assesses Drone Threat From Cuba, Report Says
headlines.summary: Cuba has acquired more than 300 military drones and is discussing plans to use them against the US base at Guantanamo Bay, according to a new assessment raising security concerns.

headlines.headline: South Korea Pursues All Options to Prevent Samsung Strike
headlines.summary: South Korea's prime minister said the government is considering all measures to prevent a potential Samsung Electronics strike that could cause unimaginable economic damage. Samsung accounts for nearly a quarter of South Korea's exports.

perspectives.angle: South Korea seeks emergency arbitration to block Samsung strike
perspectives.summary: South Korea will pursue all options including emergency arbitration to prevent a labor strike at Samsung Electronics, the country's largest employer, as the government prioritizes economic stability.

headlines.headline: Italian PM Meloni Visits Modena After Car-Ramming Incident
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni travelled to Modena after a driver with mental health problems rammed into pedestrians. Several people were injured, and Meloni cancelled a Cyprus meeting to visit the victims.

perspectives.angle: Italy PM Meloni visits Modena after car-ramming attack injures several
perspectives.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni cancelled a planned meeting with Cyprus president to travel to Modena after a car plowed into pedestrians in an incident authorities are investigating. Four bystanders tackled the knife-wielding driver after the attack.

headlines.headline: Tata-ASML Partnership to Build India's First Semiconductor Fab
headlines.summary: Tata Electronics and Dutch chip equipment maker ASML signed an agreement to build India's first front-end semiconductor fabrication plant in Gujarat, marking a major milestone in the country's push for chip manufacturing independence.

perspectives.angle: Tata and ASML Sign Major Semiconductor Deal During Modi Netherlands Visit
perspectives.summary: India's Tata Group and Dutch chip equipment giant ASML signed a semiconductor cooperation agreement as Prime Minister Modi visited the Netherlands, with talks also focusing on defense and security cooperation.

headlines.headline: Live Updates: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Iran Says It Cannot Trust Americans
headlines.summary: CBS News provides ongoing coverage of the Middle East conflict as Israel continues strikes on Lebanon and Iran declares it cannot trust the US, while FIFA officials hold constructive talks with Iranian federation about World Cup participation.

perspectives.angle: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Iran Says It Cannot Trust the US
perspectives.summary: Israel continues airstrikes on southern Lebanon despite a 45-day ceasefire extension, while Irans foreign minister says Tehran cannot trust the Americans at all as diplomatic efforts to end the war struggle.

headlines.headline: NATO tests ground combat robots near Russian border in Latvia
headlines.summary: NATO forces exercised in Latvia's dense forests this week, testing ground robots in combat scenarios near the Russian border as the alliance explores autonomous warfare capabilities.

headlines.headline: Macron Opens Africa Summit in Kenya With $27 Billion Investment Package
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced €23 billion in investments at the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, marking France's first major summit in an Anglophone African country. The move signals a shift from colonial-era dominance toward "partnership of equals" as France seeks new allies.

headlines.headline: Coal Makes Global Comeback as Iran War Cuts Off LNG Supplies
headlines.summary: Countries worldwide are returning to coal power after the Iran war effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, cutting off about 20% of global liquefied natural gas supplies. The shift is most pronounced in Asia as nations prioritize energy security over climate goals.

headlines.headline: Iran stock market to reopen Tuesday after conflict suspension
headlines.summary: Iran will reopen its stock market on Tuesday following a suspension during the conflict with the US and Israel, IRNA news agency reported. The market closure was one of the economic impacts of the Gulf War.

perspectives.angle: Iran's stock market to reopen Tuesday after war suspension
perspectives.summary: Iran will reopen its stock market on Tuesday after a suspension during the conflict with the US and Israel, IRNA news agency cited a senior official as saying, as the country begins normalizing economic activity.

headlines.headline: Palestinian President Abbas son elected to top Fatah leadership body
headlines.summary: Yasser Abbas, the 64-year-old son of aging Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas, won a seat on Fatahs central committee in elections seen as a potential succession move. The vote comes amid broader Palestinian political uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Poland blindsided by email glitch on US troop movements
headlines.summary: Poland's military chief received alerts about US troop relocations via email, revealing coordination failures as American forces shift. The incident raises questions about NATO communication protocols.

headlines.headline: Tens of thousands protest in London across two major demonstrations
headlines.summary: Massive crowds marched through central London on Saturday in separate protests over immigration policy and economic conditions, highlighting growing social tensions in Britain.

perspectives.angle: UK far-right and pro-Palestine protests draw tens of thousands in London
perspectives.summary: Tommy Robinsons Unite the Kingdom march and annual Nakba Day pro-Palestine demonstration brought large crowds to central London, with police monitoring both events.

headlines.headline: Singapore deepfake scam steals $3.8 million via fake PM video call
headlines.summary: A sophisticated scam using deepfake technology to impersonate Singapore Prime Minister Wong and President Tharman in a Zoom call conned a victim out of millions. Police have obtained footage of the conference.

headlines.headline: Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties with Nordic Nations
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Mark Carney is strengthening alliances between "middle powers" as the United States becomes a less reliable partner, part of broader efforts to counter Russian and Chinese activity in the Arctic.

perspectives.angle: Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties With Nordic Nations After Trump Threats
perspectives.summary: Canada is strengthening defense partnerships with Nordic countries as Prime Minister Mark Carney builds alliances among middle powers in response to concerns about US reliability under the Trump administration.

headlines.headline: Sierra Leone to Accept Hundreds of Deported West Africans from US
headlines.summary: The deal is the latest in a series of agreements by the Trump administration to accelerate removals of undocumented immigrants from the United States.

perspectives.angle: Sierra Leone Agrees to Accept Hundreds of West Africans Deported by US
perspectives.summary: Sierra Leone has agreed to take in hundreds of West African migrants being deported by the United States, its foreign minister told Reuters, marking the latest deportation deal by the Trump administration as it accelerates removals.

headlines.headline: Latvia Nominates New Prime Minister After Government Resignation
headlines.summary: President Edgars Rinkevics proposed opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister following Evika Silina's resignation.

headlines.headline: Young Kyiv Couple Killed in Russian Airstrike Had Hoped to Start Family
headlines.summary: Mourners in Kyiv remembered a young couple killed in a Russian airstrike, one of many civilian casualties as Russia continues its bombardment of Ukrainian cities.

headlines.headline: India Raises CNG Prices for Second Day Amid Iran War Economic Fallout
headlines.summary: CNG prices in Delhi NCR have been hiked for the second time in two days as India faces economic impacts from the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict and global oil supply concerns.

headlines.headline: Qatar and Saudi Arabia Discuss Efforts to Ease Middle East Tensions
headlines.summary: Qatar's foreign minister and Saudi counterpart held phone discussions on reducing regional tensions amid the Iran-Israel conflict, reviewing bilateral ties and coordination efforts.

headlines.headline: China Gold Recycling Industry Sees Record Growth as Bullion Freney Continues
headlines.summary: China's gold recycling sector is expanding at its fastest pace in a decade as sustained demand in the bullion market drives citizens to sell old gold for profit.

headlines.headline: Nepali Sherpa Kami Rita Summits Everest for Record 32nd Time
headlines.summary: Kami Rita Sherpa extended his own world record by summiting Mount Everest for the 32nd time, cementing his legendary status in mountaineering history.

headlines.headline: North Korean Soccer Club Arrives in South Korea for Asian Champions League
headlines.summary: Naegohyang FC became the first North Korean athletes to visit South Korea in eight years, arriving for an Asian Women's Champions League semi-final amid strained inter-Korean relations.

perspectives.angle: North Korean Women's Soccer Team Arrives in South Korea for First Time in Eight Years
perspectives.summary: North Korea's Naegohyang FC arrived in South Korea for an Asian Women's Champions League semi-final, marking the first athletic visit from the isolated state in eight years. All 7,087 public tickets sold out within a day, despite Pyongyang recently labeling Seoul its "most hostile state."

headlines.headline: Pentagon Cancels Europe Troop Deployment Amid Frustrations With NATO Over Iran War
headlines.summary: The Pentagon withdrew thousands of soldiers from a planned deployment to Poland as Trump expressed anger over European allies refusal to support the war with Iran. The move signals deepening US frustration with NATO burden-sharing as the Iran conflict strains alliance relations.

perspectives.angle: John Bolton: Iran is Europe's War Too
perspectives.summary: Former US National Security Adviser John Bolton criticized European nations like Spain for staying out of the Iran conflict, arguing that the war directly affects European security and energy interests.

headlines.headline: UAE clarifies OPEC exit was strategic, not political
headlines.summary: UAE Energy Minister Al Mazrouei said the country's withdrawal from OPEC and OPEC+ was a sovereign decision based on long-term production policy assessment, not a political move.

headlines.headline: RAF deploys new low-cost anti-drone missile system in Middle East
headlines.summary: British Typhoon jets in the Middle East are being equipped with new anti-drone missile systems to cost-effectively intercept Iranian drones. The system went from testing to deployment in under two months.

headlines.headline: US approves $25.8 billion in weapons sales to Middle East allies
headlines.summary: Secretary of State Marco Rubio approved massive weapons sales to Middle Eastern allies affected by the Iran conflict, demonstrating continued US commitment to regional partners.

headlines.headline: Armenia struggles in limbo after Nagorno-Karabakh loss
headlines.summary: Nearly three years after Azerbaijan's lightning offensive reclaimed Nagorno-Karabakh, Armenia remains in geopolitical uncertainty, with citizens told to "be ready for war" amid ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.headline: UK to scrap planned fuel tax rise amid economic pressures
headlines.summary: British finance minister Rachel Reeves plans to announce postponement of a fuel tax increase scheduled for September, as the government navigates economic challenges from the Iran conflict and global inflation.

headlines.headline: Global Oil Stockpiles Could Hit Record Lows if Hormuz Remains Closed
headlines.summary: UBS warns that world oil inventories could approach all-time lows by end of May if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, as the Iran-Israel war enters its 11th week with no resolution in sight.

headlines.headline: US MQ-9 Reaper Fleet Shrinks to 135 After 24 Losses Over Hormuz
headlines.summary: The US Air Force has lost 24 MQ-9 Reaper drones over the Strait of Hormuz since fighting with Iran began, reducing the operational fleet to 135 aircraft and raising questions about sustaining surveillance operations in contested airspace.

headlines.headline: Iran Conflict Reshapes Energy Markets, US Gas Demand Surges
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict is restructuring global energy flows as US gas demand increases and distillate inventories fall to 2005 lows. Asian emerging markets face pressure from surging oil prices and stalled US-Iran peace talks.

headlines.headline: Trump Returns from China With Stability and Stalemate
headlines.summary: The Beijing summit produced modest results by U.S.-China standards but highlighted China's benefit: after last year's trade war extremes, both nations have reverted to their familiar economic and strategic standoff. No breakthrough on the Strait of Hormuz.

perspectives.angle: Asia analysis: Trump's China visit yields stability but stalemate
perspectives.summary: Regional perspective on the Trump-Xi summit notes modest economic outcomes but highlights a return to familiar US-China economic and strategic standoff after the extremes of last year's trade war.

headlines.headline: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Has Hit a Wall With Iran
headlines.summary: Donald Trump's blustery negotiating style has won concessions from many countries, but it's not working with Iran. The approach may undermine his efforts to end the war, as Tehran resists pressure tactics that succeeded elsewhere.

perspectives.angle: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Hits Wall With Iran
perspectives.summary: After a year of concessions on tariffs and conflicts, U.S. President Donald Trump's confrontational negotiating style has met its match in Iran, where neither threats nor military action have produced the desired outcome.

headlines.headline: US Accuses Iraqi Man of Helping Iran-Backed Militia Plan Attacks
headlines.summary: An Iraqi national accused of involvement in multiple attacks against U.S. interests in Europe has been arrested and brought to the U.S. to face six terrorism-related counts. The Justice Department says he helped Iran-backed militia plan attacks in the U.S. and Europe.

perspectives.angle: US charges Iraqi man in Iran-linked terror plots against Jews
perspectives.summary: An Iraqi national has been arrested and brought to the US to face terrorism charges for involvement in multiple Iran-backed plots targeting US interests in Europe and Jewish communities.

headlines.headline: Putin to Visit Beijing for Talks With Xi Just Days After Trump Summit
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin will travel to Beijing for a two-day meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping next week, the Kremlin confirmed, a visit that comes less than 24 hours after Trump's own state visit to China and underscores the deepening Russia-China partnership.

perspectives.angle: Putin to visit Beijing days after Trump-Xi summit as China emerges as diplomatic hub
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin will meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing on May 19-20, four days after Donald Trump left China, with state media calling China the "focal point of global diplomacy" as it hosts both US and Russian leaders within a week.

headlines.headline: Iraq Oil Exports Through Hormuz Drop 90 Percent Since Iran War Began
headlines.summary: Iraq exported just 10 million barrels of oil through the Strait of Hormuz in April, down from about 93 million barrels monthly before the Iran war, the country's new oil minister said, highlighting the dramatic impact of the conflict on global energy flows.

perspectives.angle: Iraq Oil Exports Through Hormuz Drop 90% as Strait Blockade Bites
perspectives.summary: Iraq exported just 10 million barrels of oil through the Strait of Hormuz in April, down from about 93 million barrels monthly before the Iran conflict began, the country's new oil minister said, highlighting the severe impact on global energy flows.

perspectives.angle: India seeks US waiver extension for Russian oil imports amid Iran war
perspectives.summary: India has asked the U.S. to extend its waiver allowing purchases of Russian oil, Bloomberg reported, as the ongoing conflict in the Persian Gulf disrupts traditional energy supply routes.

headlines.headline: China, US, Israel Race to Dominate Laser Weapons Market
headlines.summary: Laser weapons are proliferating across the Middle East with Chinese systems spotted at Dubai airport, Israeli Iron Beam deployed to UAE, and multiple nations racing to develop directed-energy capabilities for drone defense.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Urges US to Reduce Military Aid to Zero
headlines.summary: Israel's PM told CBS that Israel should gradually wean itself off the remaining .8 billion annual US military support, amid declining American public support for Israel and ahead of 2028 aid agreement negotiations.

headlines.headline: European Defense Costs Surge 50% in Two Years
headlines.summary: Estonian Defense Minister Hanno Pevkur warned that defense product prices have jumped 50-60% since 2024, complicating NATO rearmament plans as Europe faces pressure to supply Ukraine while US attention shifts elsewhere.

headlines.headline: Venezuelan Official Alex Saab, Maduro Ally, Deported to US
headlines.summary: Venezuelan official Alex Saab, a Colombian-Venezuelan businessman and ally of former President Nicolas Maduro, was deported to the United States in a significant development involving Venezuela-US relations.

headlines.headline: Samsung Electronics Chairman Apologizes Amid Labor Tensions
headlines.summary: Samsung Electronics Chairman Jay Y. Lee apologized to customers and the public over the company wage dispute as the union prepared to resume pay talks, highlighting labor tensions at South Korea largest conglomerate.

headlines.headline: Trump Threatens Primary Challenge Against Republican Boebert
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump threatened a primary challenge against hardline conservative Representative Lauren Boebert after she campaigned for fellow Republican Thomas Massie, signaling ongoing intra-party tensions.

perspectives.angle: Trump-Targeted Senator Cassidy Faces Primary Challenge in Louisiana
perspectives.summary: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy, targeted by Trump for retribution, faces a critical primary election in Louisiana against Trump-backed and other rivals. The race is seen as a test of Trump's influence over GOP incumbents.

headlines.headline: Rising Diesel Costs from Iran War Strain US School Budgets
headlines.summary: Soaring diesel prices since the onset of the Iran war are draining already tight U.S. school district budgets, forcing some to draw from emergency financial reserves to cover transportation costs.

perspectives.angle: Diesel Costs From Iran War Drain US School Budgets
perspectives.summary: Soaring diesel prices since the onset of the Iran war are straining already tight US school district budgets, making it more expensive to bus students.

headlines.headline: France to develop ballistic missile defense with Ukraine
headlines.summary: President Zelensky announced France's readiness to develop ballistic missile defense capabilities with Ukraine, calling it an "important step" in strengthening capabilities against Russian attacks.

perspectives.angle: France Ready to Develop Ballistic Missile Defense With Ukraine, Zelensky Says
perspectives.summary: French President discussed strengthening capabilities to repel Russian attacks, with Zelensky calling the cooperation an important step in air defense development.

headlines.headline: Putin simplifies Russian citizenship for Transnistria residents
headlines.summary: Moldova's President Maia Sandu warned that Putin's decree simplifying citizenship for Transnistria residents is likely a recruitment tool to send more people to fight in Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Putin Signs Decree Simplifying Russian Citizenship for Transnistria Residents
perspectives.summary: Eligible applicants in the breakaway Moldovan region can skip Russian residency requirements, history and civic tests, and need not prove proficiency in the Russian language, signaling Moscow continued influence efforts in the region.

headlines.headline: Iran to announce Hormuz traffic management mechanism
headlines.summary: Iran is preparing a new mechanism to regulate shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, including designated routes and service fees, with European countries reportedly negotiating passage permissions.

headlines.headline: Japan to bolster sea lane defense with Southeast Asia info-sharing
headlines.summary: Japan announced plans to strengthen sea lane defense through information sharing with Southeast Asian countries, amid growing regional tensions and supply chain concerns.

headlines.headline: BlackRock weighs $5-10 billion investment in SpaceX IPO
headlines.summary: BlackRock has discussed investing $5-10 billion in SpaceX's initial public offering next month, the Information reported, marking one of the largest potential IPO investments by the asset management giant.

perspectives.angle: BlackRock weighs $5-10B investment in SpaceX IPO
perspectives.summary: BlackRock is in discussions to invest billions in SpaceX upcoming initial public offering. The investment would be one of the largest pre-IPO commitments in recent market history.

headlines.headline: Russia captures two villages in Ukraine's Kharkiv region
headlines.summary: Russian troops have taken control of the settlements of Borova and Kutkivka in Ukraine's Kharkiv region, according to Russia's RIA state news agency citing the Defence Ministry.

perspectives.angle: Russia Captures Two Villages in Ukraine's Kharkiv Region
perspectives.summary: Russian forces have taken control of Borova and Kutkivka settlements in Kharkiv, marking continued territorial gains as Moscow presses its offensive in eastern Ukraine.

headlines.headline: ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in US-Nigeria Joint Operation
headlines.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint US-Nigerian military operation in northeastern Nigeria. President Trump and Nigerian President Tinubu confirmed the operation, marking a significant blow to the terrorist organization.

headlines.headline: IDF Infantry Commander Killed by Hezbollah Drone in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: Captain Maoz Israel Recanati, 24, was killed by a Hezbollah drone attack in southern Lebanon. He was set to marry next month. The IDF struck over 100 targets in response.

headlines.headline: Jerusalem Governorate Warns of Israeli Plan to Seize Properties Near Al-Aqsa
headlines.summary: Palestinian authorities warn that Israel plans to approve seizure of Palestinian properties in the Bab al-Silsila neighborhood near Al-Aqsa Mosque on Sunday.

headlines.headline: Trump Not Optimistic on Jimmy Lai Release After Raising Case With Xi
headlines.summary: US President Trump says he raised the case of imprisoned Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai with Chinese President Xi, but is not optimistic about his release. Lai was sentenced to 20 years under Hong Kong national security law.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Urges Lebanon to Abandon Peace Illusions With Israel
headlines.summary: Hezbollah warned Lebanese authorities against direct negotiations with Israel as Washington pushes forward with a new round of talks between Beirut and Tel Aviv.

headlines.headline: Israel kills Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza strike
headlines.summary: An Israeli airstrike killed the head of Hamas armed wing, the most senior Hamas official killed since the October ceasefire. Israel says he helped plan the Oct 7 attacks.

perspectives.angle: Hamas Gaza Commander Killed by Israel, Testing Ceasefire
perspectives.summary: Israel assassinated Hamas top commander Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza. The group pledged to adapt operations following the killing, which comes amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: FIFA Officials Meet Iranian FA to Discuss World Cup Participation
headlines.summary: FIFA Secretary-General Mattias Grafstrom held constructive talks with Iran's Football Federation in Istanbul, offering reassurance about the country's participation in this year's World Cup amid concerns about Iran's status following recent regional conflicts.

perspectives.angle: FIFA Officials to Meet Iranian FA Over World Cup Participation
perspectives.summary: FIFA Secretary-General Mattias Grafstrom will meet Iranian Football Federation officials in Istanbul to offer reassurance over Iran participation in the upcoming World Cup amid the ongoing Iran-US conflict.

headlines.headline: Latvian President Proposes Opposition Lawmaker as Next Prime Minister
headlines.summary: Latvian President Edgars Rinkevics nominated opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister after Evika Silina's resignation. The move comes as Baltic states strengthen defense ties amid regional security concerns.

perspectives.angle: Latvia faces political uncertainty as president names opposition lawmaker as PM candidate
perspectives.summary: Latvian President Edgars Rinkevics proposed opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister after Evika Silina resigned, amid regional security concerns.

perspectives.angle: Latvian Prime Minister Resigns Over Ukrainian Drone Incident Handling
perspectives.summary: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned, triggering coalition government collapse months before October elections, amid controversy over handling of Ukrainian drone incidents.

headlines.headline: Romania finds unexploded projectile near Ukraine border
headlines.summary: NATO member Romania discovered an unexploded projectile on property near its Ukraine border, highlighting spillover risks from the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran War Worth Economic Pain, Say Rural Trump Voters
headlines.summary: Despite rising gas prices and economic strain from the Iran conflict, rural Colorado voters remain steadfast in their support for Trump administration policies.

headlines.headline: Colombia Mayor Shot Dead During Election Campaign
headlines.summary: A former mayor allied with presidential candidate Abelardo de La Espriella was assassinated in central Colombia amid escalating election violence.

headlines.headline: S&P Upgrades Nigeria on Improving Macroeconomic Profile
headlines.summary: Credit ratings agency S&P upgraded Nigeria long-term sovereign rating to B from B-, citing improving creditworthiness and economic reforms.

headlines.headline: Iran Plans Fees for Ships Passing Through Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran is preparing to charge fees for commercial vessels transiting the strategic Strait of Hormuz amid tensions with the US and Israel. Only vessels from cooperative countries would benefit under the proposed mechanism, tightening control over a critical oil transit route.

headlines.headline: Israel Threatens Defamation Lawsuit Against NYT Over Sexual Abuse Report
headlines.summary: Israel's Prime Minister Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Sa'ar announced plans to sue the New York Times over a Nicholas Kristof essay alleging sexual abuse of Palestinian prisoners. The Times defended the reporting as rigorously fact-checked, while media law experts questioned whether such a lawsuit by a government could proceed.

headlines.headline: Rwandan genocide suspect Lucien Kabuga dies at 93 in UN custody
headlines.summary: Lucien Kabuga, accused of financing the 1994 genocide in Rwanda, has died in custody at age 93. He was one of the last remaining suspects from the international tribunal.

headlines.headline: UK Prime Minister Starmer set to approve $24B defense spending increase
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer is expected to approve an 18 billion pound increase in defense spending as he faces political pressure. The Times reports the increase comes amid global security concerns.

headlines.headline: Eight killed as freight train hits bus in Thailand
headlines.summary: At least eight people died and 32 were injured when a freight train struck a bus at a rail crossing in Bangkok, igniting a fire that engulfed the vehicle.

headlines.headline: Russians Worry More About Strikes at Home Than Front Line for First Time Since 2022
headlines.summary: A new poll shows shifting public anxiety inside Russia, with growing concern about domestic attacks versus battlefield risks as Ukrainian drone strikes intensify.

perspectives.angle: Poll: Russians fear strikes at home more than front line for first time since 2022
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes deep inside Russia have shifted Russian public concern from battlefield developments to domestic security, according to a new poll.

headlines.headline: US-Israel Preparing for Possible Renewal of Strikes on Iran, Report Says
headlines.summary: Haaretz reports preparations underway for potential new strikes against Iranian targets as diplomatic negotiations remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Warns Russia May Attack NATO from Belarus
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president says Moscow is seeking to draw Belarus deeper into the war and weighing plans to attack Ukraine's north or a NATO country from Belarusian territory.

perspectives.angle: Zelenskiy Warns Russia May Attack NATO From Belarus
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy stated that Russia is considering plans to attack Ukraine's north or a NATO country from Belarusian territory, drawing Belarus deeper into the war.

headlines.headline: Consumers Sue Amazon Over Unlawful Trump Tariff Costs
headlines.summary: Amazon faces lawsuit from consumers seeking refunds for higher prices resulting from tariffs the Supreme Court later ruled were unlawfully imposed by President Trump.

headlines.headline: US and Brazil ethanol exports surge as countries seek fuel alternatives
headlines.summary: The world's two largest ethanol producers are seeing export increases as consuming countries seek alternative fuel sources while the Strait of Hormuz crisis continues.

headlines.headline: China criticizes US Hormuz resolution at UN as timing and content questioned
headlines.summary: China's UN ambassador criticized a proposed US-Bahraini resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, saying the content and timing were inappropriate and passing it would not be helpful.

headlines.headline: Italy's Meloni wins European backing for third-country migrant return hubs
headlines.summary: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni hailed a shift in European migration policy as her controversial approach to offshore migrant processing gains support across the Council of Europe.

headlines.headline: UAE States All Actions Were Defensive Measures to Protect Sovereignty
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates foreign ministry issued a statement saying all actions taken by the Gulf country were defensive measures aimed at protecting its sovereignty, civilians, and vital infrastructure amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Eurovision Song Contest Final Proceeds Amid Gaza Boycott
headlines.summary: Five countries have withdrawn from the Eurovision Song Contest final over Israel participation, with the event taking place against the backdrop of ongoing Middle East tensions.

perspectives.angle: Spain PM backs Eurovision boycott, says silence on Gaza "not an option"
perspectives.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez defended Spain's boycott of Eurovision over Israel's participation, saying it puts the country "on the right side of history" and warning against "double standards" compared to Russia's exclusion.

headlines.headline: US Reportedly Preparing to Indict Former Cuban Leader Raul Castro
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department is reportedly preparing to criminally indict former Cuban leader Raul Castro in the coming days, marking a significant development in US-Cuba relations.

headlines.headline: US Proposes Dramatic Colorado River Water Cuts Amid Drought
headlines.summary: US government plans could cut up to 40% of water supplies to Arizona, California and Nevada. The drought-stricken Colorado River basin faces unprecedented pressure from climate change and overuse.

headlines.headline: OpenAI Signs Deal to Provide ChatGPT Plus to All Maltese Citizens
headlines.summary: OpenAI partners with Malta's government to give all residents access to ChatGPT Plus for one year following AI training course. First nationwide AI access program of its kind.

headlines.headline: Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days
headlines.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by 45 days to enable further diplomatic progress, the U.S. State Department announced, even as Israel continues strikes it says fall outside the truce agreement.

perspectives.angle: Lebanon-Israel Ceasefire Extended 45 Days Despite Ongoing Strikes
perspectives.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed in Washington to extend their ceasefire by 45 days to advance political negotiations, though Israel continued airstrikes in southern Lebanon immediately after the agreement. The talks aim for a broader settlement but military actions continue.

perspectives.angle: CBS News Live: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Iran Distrusts US
perspectives.summary: CBS News provides ongoing coverage of Middle East tensions as Israel conducts strikes in Lebanon while Iran expresses deep mistrust of American involvement in the conflict.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Talks With Iranian Officials
headlines.summary: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visits Tehran amid regional tensions, as Pakistan navigates relations with Iran during the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Moscow Signals Frustration as US-Russia Contacts Remain Difficult
headlines.summary: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Ryabkov indicates that US-Russia diplomatic contacts remain slow and difficult amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in Joint US-Nigeria Operation
headlines.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint operation by US and Nigerian forces in northeastern Nigeria. Both presidents Trump and Tinubu confirmed the elimination of the terrorist leader.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Presses US for Arms Sales After Trump Undecided on Future Deliveries
headlines.summary: Taiwan's government emphasized that US arms sales are essential for regional peace and deterrence, following President Trump's statement that he has not decided on future weapons sales to the island.

headlines.headline: Council of Europe Approves Special Tribunal to Try Russian Leadership for Aggression
headlines.summary: The Council of Europe has approved steps to establish a Nuremberg-style tribunal to prosecute Russia's leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine. 36 countries and the EU have committed to supporting the Special Tribunal.

headlines.headline: Turkish Prosecutors Expand Istanbul Corruption Investigation Targeting Opposition Municipality
headlines.summary: Turkish police detained 12 suspects as part of an expanding corruption investigation into alleged irregularities in municipal tenders linked to Istanbul's opposition-run metropolitan municipality. The probe has intensified political tensions.

headlines.headline: UK Prime Minister to approve 24 billion dollar defense spending increase
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer is expected to approve an 18 billion pound defense spending boost as he faces political challenges. The increase comes amid broader European security concerns and NATO commitments.

headlines.headline: UK Markets Slide on Political Turmoil and Oil Price Fears
headlines.summary: British equities and bonds fell as investors weighed a potential leadership challenge to PM Keir Starmer alongside mounting concerns about oil-driven inflation from the Iran-Hormuz crisis.

headlines.headline: Trump Warns Taiwan: No Blank Check from US Military
